ddls:
CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "twitter_id" TEXT /* Twitter ID of the user */,
  "date_of_data_collection" TEXT /* Date when the data was collected */,
  "followers" INTEGER /* Number of followers the user has */,
  "following" INTEGER /* Number of accounts the user is following */,
  "activity_level" TEXT /* Level of activity (e.g., None, Low, Medium, High) */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "user_bio" TEXT /* User's bio or profile description */,
  "user_location" TEXT /* User's location */,
  "user_website" TEXT /* User's website URL */,
  "user_join_date" TEXT /* Date the user joined Twitter */,
  "user_profile_image_url" TEXT /* URL of the user's profile image */,
  "user_is_verified" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the user is verified */,
  "user_is_protected" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the user's account is protected */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "activity_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each activity log */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "log_date" TEXT /* Date of the activity */,
  "activity_type" TEXT /* Type of activity (e.g., post, like, retweet) */,
  "activity_count" INTEGER /* Count of the activity */,
  "activity_source" TEXT /* Source of the activity (e.g., web, mobile app) */,
  "activity_text" TEXT /* Text content of the activity */,
  "activity_media_url" TEXT /* URL of any media (image, video) associated with the activity */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_activity_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "engagement_metrics" (
  "engagement_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each engagement metric */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "like_count" INTEGER /* Number of likes the user has received */,
  "retweet_count" INTEGER /* Number of retweets the user has received */,
  "reply_count" INTEGER /* Number of replies the user has received */,
  "engagement_score" REAL /* Overall engagement score */,
  "engagement_date" TEXT /* Date of the engagement */,
  "engagement_type" TEXT /* Type of engagement (e.g., post, like, retweet, reply) */,
  "engagement_source" TEXT /* Source of the engagement (e.g., web, mobile app) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("engagement_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_engagement_metrics_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_segments" (
  "segment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user segment */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "segment_type" TEXT /* Type of segment (e.g., location, interest, influence) */,
  "segment_value" TEXT /* Value of the segment (e.g., New York, Cryptocurrency, Influencer) */,
  "segment_start_date" TEXT /* Date the user was added to the segment */,
  "segment_end_date" TEXT /* Date the user was removed from the segment (if applicable) */,
  "segment_source" TEXT /* Source of the segmentation data (e.g., manual, algorithmic) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("segment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_segments_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "trends" (
  "trend_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each trend */,
  "trend_name" TEXT /* Name of the trend */,
  "trend_start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the trend */,
  "trend_end_date" TEXT /* End date of the trend */,
  "trend_description" TEXT /* Description of the trend */,
  "trend_score" REAL /* Score of the trend based on popularity and engagement */,
  "trend_peak_date" TEXT /* Date when the trend peaked */,
  "trend_volume" INTEGER /* Volume of the trend (e.g., number of tweets) */,
  "trend_geography" TEXT /* Geographical spread of the trend */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("trend_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sentiment_analysis" (
  "sentiment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sentiment analysis */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "post_id" INTEGER /* ID of the post */,
  "sentiment_score" REAL /* Sentiment score (e.g., -1 to 1) */,
  "sentiment_type" TEXT /* Type of sentiment (e.g., positive, negative, neutral) */,
  "sentiment_analysis_date" TEXT /* Date when the sentiment analysis was performed */,
  "sentiment_confidence" REAL /* Confidence level of the sentiment analysis */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sentiment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sentiment_analysis_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "competitor_analysis" (
  "analysis_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each competitor analysis */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "competitor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the competitor */,
  "comparison_date" TEXT /* Date of the comparison */,
  "follower_difference" INTEGER /* Difference in followers */,
  "following_difference" INTEGER /* Difference in following */,
  "engagement_difference" REAL /* Difference in engagement score */,
  "analysis_source" TEXT /* Source of the competitor analysis data (e.g., manual, automated tool) */,
  "analysis_notes" TEXT /* Notes or comments about the analysis */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("analysis_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_competitor_analysis_competitor_id FOREIGN KEY ("competitor_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_competitor_analysis_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users_followers" (
  "follow_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each follow relationship */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user being followed */,
  "follower_id" INTEGER /* ID of the follower */,
  "follow_date" TEXT /* Date when the follow occurred */,
  "follower_is_verified" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the follower is verified */,
  "follower_is_protected" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the follower's account is protected */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("follow_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_followers_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_followers_follower_id FOREIGN KEY ("follower_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users_following" (
  "follow_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each follow relationship */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "following_id" INTEGER /* ID of the account being followed */,
  "follow_date" TEXT /* Date when the follow occurred */,
  "following_is_verified" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the followed user is verified */,
  "following_is_protected" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the followed user's account is protected */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("follow_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_following_following_id FOREIGN KEY ("following_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_following_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
